message:
test(readme): PeriodFreq::parse/::alias round-trip across 9 variants

Pins frankenpandas-fd90.55: the pandas-parity surface for
PeriodFreq has 9 variants (Annual/Quarterly/Monthly/Weekly/Daily/
Business/Hourly/Minutely/Secondly) and documented parse() / alias()
methods, but neither method was covered by integration tests.

The new readme_periodfreq_parse_alias_round_trip case asserts:

* alias() returns the canonical pandas one-letter alias for every
  variant ("A", "Q", "M", "W", "D", "B", "H", "T", "S").
* parse(canonical) round-trips back to the same variant for each
  of the 9 cases.
* parse() is case-insensitive — same canonical input lower-cased
  also round-trips, matching pandas' behavior.
* Long-form aliases parse correctly: "ANNUAL", "YEARLY", and "Y"
  all map to PeriodFreq::Annual; "MONTHLY" to Monthly; "MIN" to
  Minutely.
* Garbage input ("garbage", "") returns None rather than panicking
  or silently picking a default.

A regression in either method would otherwise only surface through
downstream pandas-parity tests, where the failure mode is far
removed from the broken contract. This pins both methods in one
place.
